design_philosophy: A calm, product-first enterprise LMS dashboard that uses progressive disclosure and role-based surfacing. The structural UI is built on Dark Navy and White to convey trust, while Crimson Red acts as a high-signal action color, tying into the brand logo. Typography blends editorial authority (Cabinet Grotesk) with technical clarity (IBM Plex).

components.button_primary: bg-[#E11D48] hover:bg-[#BE123C] text-white font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-sm transition-colors
components.button_secondary: bg-white border border-slate-300 text-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-50 font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-sm transition-colors
components.topbar: bg-white border-b border-slate-200 h-16 flex items-center px-6
components.badge_course: bg-slate-100 text-slate-700 px-2 py-1 text-xs font-medium rounded

accessibility.contrast_strategy: Ensure 4.5:1 ratio on text. Primary red (#E11D48) on white passes AA.
accessibility.testing_attributes: All interactive elements MUST have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='course-card-resume')

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the Light theme with the dark navy sidebar and crimson red accents. Do not introduce purple, teal, or neon gradients.
Use Cabinet Grotesk for headings and IBM Plex Sans for body text. Load these via Google Fonts.
Build the dashboard using a CSS Grid strategy. The main content should have a calm, spacious layout (p-6 or p-8 inside cards).
Implement the 'Control Room' grid for the Admin Dashboard: top metrics in 1-col widgets, charts in 2-col/3-col widgets.
Use @phosphor-icons/react for a clean, professional, enterprise feel.
Ensure the sidebar supports role-based navigation (Super Admin, Trainer, Learner).
Use real text and the provided images from media keys. No placeholder images like via.placeholder.com.
Make sure all components are highly accessible, supporting keyboard navigation and screen readers.
Implement login handling differentiating AD001+ patterns for Admins vs 1001-1500 patterns for learners.
